# Questions in Livestream 252

Live Stream #252: The Golden Sound Fox

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:16</a> | Will David make a video game in 3D? | No. He said one video game per lifetime is enough and that a 3D project would require more energy, time, and infrastructure than he has for it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:23</a> | What language was Egg Hunter developed in? | It was developed in a mix of Ren'Py and Python. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=906" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:06</a> | Are women more prone to heresy than men? | He said not as individuals in any simple sense, though he argued group dynamics can affect how error spreads. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=1059" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:39</a> | In Exodus 14:22, could a wind have made the waters appear like a wall on either side? | He said `wall` should be read in its ancient sense and not imagined as vertical movie-style water towers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=1213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:13</a> | Would a name for `Ramesses, son of Ahmose` actually look like `Ramesses`? | No. He said the forms look similar at first glance but differ in an important grammatical element. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=1493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:53</a> | How did you approach Exodus 32 to reach your conclusions about what is happening in the text? | He said he starts with the text itself, then compares it with cultural context and related passages before drawing conclusions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=2035" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:55</a> | Is there any meaningful difference between resurrection and revivification? | If someone is truly dead, he said there is no practical difference in the basic claim that life has been restored. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=2505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:45</a> | Why insist the Exodus 32 idol had to be gold and golden in appearance? | He said Sinai supplied more than gold alone, but the golden-calf language still matters and should not be flattened away. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=2726" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:26</a> | Why do some scholars think Exodus 32 was written after Jeroboam's calf incident in 1 Kings 12? | He said they reverse the dependence because of critical assumptions, but he rejected that move and treated it as poor reasoning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3053" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:53</a> | Did the Egyptians have hammocks? | No. He said they could have made them, but the Egyptian environment did not naturally lend itself to the usual setup. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:47</a> | What are the best books or commentaries for studying Isaiah? | He said there are many good options and named works by John Oswalt, John Goldingay, H. G. M. Williamson, Geoffrey Grogan, Edward Young, Barry Webb, and J. Alec Motyer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:05</a> | What are your views on the 1972 Toronto egregore experiment? | He dismissed it as methodologically flawed and ultimately inconclusive because it lacked proper controls and was not double blind. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3423" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:03</a> | How much freedom did ordinary Egyptians have to leave their work or village? | He said very little; an ordinary farmer could not simply move away without a good reason and permission. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3711" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:51</a> | Would Egyptian craftsmen have lived in guarded, sequestered villages, almost like a police state? | Many working specialists probably did live in controlled settlements, though he did not think every Egyptian lived under that exact pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=3874" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:34</a> | What do you think about robots built for human companionship? | He called it a bad idea, arguing that artificial companions cannot replace real human relationships and may deepen relational distortion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=4407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:27</a> | Are you a cessationist or a continuationist, and why? | He identified as a continuationist, holding that spiritual gifts continue beyond the apostolic age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=4475" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:35</a> | Does Joshua 6:25's `to this day` wording mean Joshua was written before Rahab died? | Not necessarily. He said the phrase can mean `ever since`, while still allowing that Joshua may preserve material from around the early monarchy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=4810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:10</a> | Will we remember things from this life in heaven? | Yes, but he said those memories will lose their present weight and urgency in the fullness of eternal life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=4952" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:32</a> | What do you think manna was during the wilderness years? | He suggested something like a fungus or desert truffle analogue rather than treating the description as random or purely symbolic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=5060" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:20</a> | What is the best book for Egyptian foods and table etiquette? | He said table etiquette is not really the right category for most ancient Egyptians, but recommended Lise Manniche's work on Egyptian herbs and food. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=5225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:05</a> | What is the best book on Egyptian symbols and symbolism? | He said the topic is too broad for one dependable book and should be narrowed to particular symbols. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=6133" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:13</a> | What are spiritual gifts? | He described them as abilities given by God to believers for the service and strengthening of the church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=6946" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:46</a> | Could a brickmaker, metalworker, or fig knocker bully an average Egyptian farmer? | Usually not. He said those workers generally occupied different contexts, with metalworkers sequestered, brickmakers often enslaved, and fig knockers tied to seasonal orchard work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=7033" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:13</a> | What examples in Joshua suggest an early monarchic period? | He could not retrieve the specifics live, but said the structures of Joshua and Judges show similarities and that some material intimates an early monarchic setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=7651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:31</a> | What evidence supports Sinai being savannah-like at the time of the Exodus? | He pointed to mining-era evidence, rainfall patterns, vegetation needs, and broader environmental indicators that the region once supported far more pasture than today. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=8288" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:08</a> | What authority or responsibility did Adam have to confront the serpent in Eden? | He said Adam did have dominion and responsibility to protect the garden order, which means he should have resisted rather than passively allowing the encounter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=8525" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:05</a> | Could anyone move up in Egyptian society despite its sequestered structure? | Yes, but it was uncommon. He said some slaves could rise to high office or become scribes, while movement from farmer to merchant would be much harder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=8789" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:29</a> | How can you use the marriage stele of Ramesses II if Kim Ryholt dates the hieroglyphic to the Persian period? | He rejected Ryholt's judgment here and said the stele can still be used because he considers that dating assessment unreliable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=9266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:26</a> | What is the difference between genealogy and ethnography? | He said genealogies explain descent and posterity, while ethnography is used to frame peoples and cultures in moral or social terms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=9786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:06</a> | Were the Exodus Hebrews the same linguistic grouping as the Amorites? | Yes. He said they were Amorites linguistically, culturally, and genetically in the broad sense he was using. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=9902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:02</a> | Were Amorites Hebrews who rejected Israelite religion, or something else? | He said no; Hebrews were a splinter group from the broader Amorite world, while Amorites as a whole had their own religions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:43</a> | Who is a good underrated church father to study? | He recommended Clement the First and Chrysostom as rewarding early Christian writers to study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:08</a> | If James thought Philo was scripture, what does that mean about Philo's Logos theology? | He said `graphe` can simply mean writing, and even if an author is cited, that does not make everything the author wrote inspired or true. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:42</a> | Have you changed your opinion on translating Genesis 1:1? | No. He said he still rejects the introductory subordinate-clause translation and has not changed his position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:47</a> | How does the clan view of genealogies explain Noah's ages around his sons and death? | He said the clan view treats the named figure as standing for a clan, so the long lifespan can describe the clan's duration rather than one individual life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10689" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:09</a> | Is the church a visible institution like the Levitical priesthood and temple system? | He said there is visible order and practice, but not a one-to-one institutional parallel with the temple priesthood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=10959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:39</a> | What is the relationship between the church and Israel, not just the modern country? | He described the church as connected to Israel's covenantal identity rather than as a detached replacement with no continuity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=11046" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:06</a> | Do people misuse Romans 9:6-8? | Yes. He said the passage can be misused through anti-Semitic bias, but it still speaks of children of promise and Israel's eventual grafting back in. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=11282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:02</a> | Why do all interlinears use Strong's Concordance? | He said Strong's is used so often because it is public domain, free, and cheap, while better lexical tools are expensive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:25</a> | Is the Ezra in Nehemiah 8 the same Ezra from the book of Ezra? | Yes. He said the identification is straightforward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12219" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:39</a> | Does Nehemiah 8:2 mean the event happened on a Sunday? | Not from that wording. He said `first day` means the first day of the seventh month, not necessarily the first day of the week. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:00</a> | In 1 Kings 1:50, what does it mean that Adonijah took hold of the horns of the altar? | He explained the altar horns as part of the altar and said grasping them was a claim for sanctuary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:55</a> | Did Adonijah request Abishag because she had laid with David? | Yes. He said Abishag's connection to David made the request a political attempt to challenge Solomon's throne. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12736" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:16</a> | How did 1 Kings 2:27 fulfill prophecy? | He said Abiathar's removal fulfilled the word against Eli's house by ending that clan's hold on the priesthood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=12830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:50</a> | Why did Joab take hold of the horns of the altar in 1 Kings 2:28? | He said Joab was seeking sanctuary, assuming that killing him at the altar would desecrate it with human blood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=13032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:12</a> | Does Solomon in 1 Kings 2:46 sound like a man of blood? | He said Solomon was eliminating rivals in a way kings commonly did, while `man of blood` more specifically describes a pattern of needless warfare like David's. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=13810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:10</a> | In 1 Kings 3:3, were Solomon's sacrifices to Yahweh or pagan gods? | They were to Yahweh, but in an unprescribed and aberrant high-place setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=13942" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:22</a> | If Solomon was wise, why did he still fall into sin? | He said wisdom does not remove sinfulness; even wise people remain sinners and can make sinful choices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/QXhwWyCR6hc?t=14128" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:28</a> | What is the most accurate depiction of the Exodus in film or television? | He chose Cecil B. DeMille's version as the best of the available screen depictions, while still treating all dramatizations as limited. |
